title: selinum conium
intro: Selinum conium, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a biennial or perennial plant belonging to the carrot family, characterized by its lacy, white flowers and fern-like foliage. The medicinal properties of Selinum conium include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate coughs, colds, and other respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Selinum conium can be made into teas, infusions, tinctures, or poultices, and are often used to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and promote relaxation.
